target.title: fix(mcp-stdio): propagate proxy env vars to child processes
target.body:
## Summary

MCP stdio child processes silently lose proxy configuration (`HTTPS_PROXY`, `HTTP_PROXY`, `NO_PROXY`) and `NODE_OPTIONS` from the gateway process. This breaks MCP servers in proxy-only environments (sandboxed containers, corporate networks, OpenShell).

**Root cause:** The MCP SDK's `StdioClientTransport` intentionally restricts env inheritance to 6 vars on Linux. `resolveStdioMcpServerLaunchConfig` passes user-configured env straight through without compensating for this restriction.

**Fix:** Merge proxy-related env vars from `process.env` as lowest-priority defaults before passing to `StdioClientTransport`. User-configured env values always take precedence. When no proxy vars are set, behavior is unchanged (`env` remains `undefined`).

### Changes

- **`src/agents/mcp-stdio.ts`**: Added `getProxyEnvDefaults()` to collect proxy env vars from the gateway process. Modified `resolveStdioMcpServerLaunchConfig` to merge these as lowest-priority defaults with user-configured env.
- **`src/agents/mcp-stdio.test.ts`**: 9 tests covering proxy propagation, user override precedence, merge behavior, empty-string filtering, and no-op when no proxy vars are set.

### How it works

```js
// Proxy vars from gateway (lowest priority)
const proxyDefaults = getProxyEnvDefaults();
// User config from openclaw.json (highest priority)
const userEnv = toMcpStringRecord(raw.env);
// Merge: user wins on conflict
const mergedEnv = { ...proxyDefaults, ...userEnv };
```

### Prior art

Same class of fix as #62878 (Slack Socket Mode proxy support), which was merged for the same reason — child processes not honoring ambient proxy configuration.

Fixes #65525
Fixes #65526

## Test plan

- [x] `getProxyEnvDefaults` returns empty object when no proxy vars set
- [x] `getProxyEnvDefaults` collects upper and lower case proxy vars
- [x] `getProxyEnvDefaults` collects `NO_PROXY` and `NODE_OPTIONS`
- [x] `getProxyEnvDefaults` ignores empty string values
- [x] Gateway proxy vars propagated when user env not configured
- [x] User-configured env overrides gateway proxy vars
- [x] Gateway proxy vars merge with user env (user wins on conflict)
- [x] `env` is `undefined` when no proxy vars and no user env (no behavior change)
- [x] All 9 tests pass: `npx vitest run src/agents/mcp-stdio.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts`
- [x] Existing `mcp-transport-config.test.ts` tests still pass
- [x] TypeScript compiles cleanly (`tsc --noEmit` — no errors in `mcp-stdio`)
- [x] `oxlint` reports 0 warnings, 0 errors

target.title: fix(cron): deduplicate main-session systemEvent in heartbeat prompt (issue #44922)
target.body:
## Summary

When a cron job with  and  fires, the same reminder text appears twice in the session context:

1. The cron service injects the systemEvent text directly into the session as a system message
2. A subsequent heartbeat triggers with  and wraps the same text in a cron reminder prompt

This results in the model seeing the same content twice, creating confusion in the conversation history.

## Root Cause

In , main-session cron jobs enqueue a system event with . Then in , the  function scans pending events and wraps any event with a contextKey starting with "cron:" in a reminder prompt via .

For  jobs, the raw systemEvent is already injected into conversation history - the wrapper is redundant.

## Fix

- Tag main-session cron systemEvents with a special contextKey ending in 
- Skip the cron wrapper prompt for events with this tag in 

## Testing

Added regression test: "does not duplicate main-session cron systemEvent via reminder wrapper (issue #44922)"

All existing cron + heartbeat tests pass.

target.title: fix(acp): add distributed session locking with fail-closed redis fallback
target.body:
## Summary
Adds distributed ACP session locking with Redis support and fail-closed behavior when Redis is configured but unavailable.

## What changed
- added `SessionLockManager` abstraction
- added `LocalSessionLockManager`
- added `RedisSessionLockManager`
- added `FailClosedSessionLockManager`
- wired lock acquire/release/renew into `tryDispatchAcpReply`
- skip ACP dispatch with `acp_execution_locked` when lock is contended or acquire fails
- documented:
  - `OPENCLAW_ACP_SESSION_LOCK_REDIS_URL`
  - `OPENCLAW_ACP_SESSION_LOCK_TTL_MS`
- added unit + dispatch integration tests

## Locking behavior
- no Redis configured → local in-process lock manager
- Redis configured and healthy → Redis lock manager
- Redis configured but init fails → fail closed, no fallback to local
- release only if owner matches
- renew only if owner matches
- renew is best-effort during long-running turns

## Validation
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `pnpm tsgo`
- `pnpm vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/session-lock-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts`

target.title: feat(macos): ExecuTorch Parakeet-TDT STT for Talk Mode + model-plugin runtime
target.body:
## Summary

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1bf90673-b796-410c-a87f-2294cbbeaca8

- **Problem:** No on-device STT option for macOS Talk Mode; executorch extension was single-model and injected technical text (e.g. "STT: ExecuTorch Parakeet-TDT") into the prompt and chat UI; PermissionManager could crash when `UNUserNotificationCenter.current()` was used outside an app bundle (e.g. Xcode run).
- **Why it matters:** Users get optional offline Parakeet-TDT STT for Talk Mode; the model no longer echoes STT/backend names; chat shows only the spoken transcript; crashes in non-.app contexts (tests/debug) are avoided.
- **What changed:** (1) ExecuTorch Parakeet-TDT Metal runtime as optional Talk Mode STT backend. (2) executorch refactored to model-plugin layout (registry, parakeet plugin, runtime-config). (3) Talk Mode prompt no longer includes STT backend name; chat UI shows only transcript via `TalkPromptBuilder.displayText(fromPrompt:)`. (4) PermissionManager guards notification APIs with `canQueryUserNotificationCenter` (main bundle is `.app`).
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Default Talk Mode STT remains Apple Speech; gateway chat protocol and session format unchanged; no new gateway APIs; other channels/integrations untouched.
- **Platform support**: While this diff focuses on macOS (Metal backend), OpenClaw's native app also supports Windows and Linux with both CUDA and CPU backends for on-device inference.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations (executorch plugin, media/STT)
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX (macOS Talk Mode, chat bubble display)
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- **New:** Optional ExecuTorch Parakeet-TDT STT for Talk Mode (enable via plugin + `defaults write … openclaw.talkSttBackend executorch`). CLI: `openclaw executorch setup|status|transcribe`.
- **Changed:** Talk Mode user message in chat shows only the transcript; model no longer sees "STT: ExecuTorch Parakeet-TDT" in the prompt.
- **Fixed:** No crash when checking notification permission outside an app bundle (e.g. Xcode, tests).

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? **No** (existing mic/speech permissions; optional plugin).
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**.
- New/changed network calls? **No** (on-device STT; executorch setup may download model/runtime from Hugging Face per README).
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** (new CLI subcommands under existing plugin).
- Data access scope changed? **No**.
- If any Yes, explain: N/A.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (Apple Silicon for Parakeet Metal).
- Runtime/container: Node 22+, macOS app.
- Model/provider: N/A for STT; model used for Talk Mode reply unchanged.
- Integration/channel: executorch plugin; macOS Talk Mode.
- Relevant config (redacted): `plugins.entries.executorch.enabled`, `openclaw.talkSttBackend` (macOS defaults).

### Steps

1. Enable executorch plugin; run `openclaw executorch setup` then `openclaw executorch status` (all OK).
2. Set `defaults write <bundleId> openclaw.talkSttBackend executorch`; relaunch app; use Talk Mode and speak.
3. Confirm chat user bubble shows only transcript (e.g. "Hello, can you hear me?") and reply does not mention ExecuTorch.
4. Run macOS app from Xcode (or run tests that hit PermissionManager); confirm no crash on notification permission check.

### Expected

- Talk Mode uses Parakeet when configured; chat shows transcript only; no PermissionManager crash in non-.app runs.

### Actual

- (Match expected after applying this PR.)

## Evidence

- [x] Unit tests: TalkPromptBuilderTests (displayText strips prefix; STT not in prompt).
- [x] Manual: Talk Mode with executorch; chat transcript-only display; PermissionManager under Xcode/tests.
- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after (N/A for feature).
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant): N/A.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:** Talk Mode with executorch backend; chat shows only transcript; executorch status/setup/transcribe; no crash when running from Xcode with notification permission check.
- **Edge cases checked:** Voice directive hint on/off; interruption line; non–Talk Mode user messages unchanged.
- **What I did not verify:** Other platforms (iOS/Android); long-run stability; upgrade from a repo state before this branch.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes** (executorch optional; default STT unchanged; displayText only affects messages that match Talk Mode prompt pattern).
- Config/env changes? **Yes** (optional plugin config; optional `openclaw.talkSttBackend` on macOS).
- Migration needed? **No** (new/optional features).
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- **How to disable/revert:** Disable executorch plugin; or set `openclaw.talkSttBackend` to default/Apple; revert PR.
- **Files/config to restore:** `plugins.entries.executorch.enabled false`; remove or unset `openclaw.talkSttBackend`.
- **Known bad symptoms:** Talk Mode STT fails if executorch enabled but native addon or runtime missing; follow `extensions/executorch/README.md` setup.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** Parakeet runtime/model mismatch or load failure when executorch is enabled.
  - **Mitigation:** Plugin logs and `executorch status`; README troubleshooting; plugin can be disabled without affecting rest of app.
- **Risk:** Display text stripping could mis-strip if user message literally starts with the same Talk Mode header.
  - **Mitigation:** Pattern is specific (starts with "Talk Mode active." and has "\n\n"); normal typing won’t match.
- **Risk:** None for PermissionManager change (narrow guard, no behavior change for shipped .app).

target.title: feat: Update Tailscale Gateway w/ custom control server configuration, validation & test coverage
target.body:
- Introduced `gateway.tailscale.controlUrl` for connecting to self-hosted Tailscale-compatible servers like Headscale.
- Updated validation to reject `serve` and `funnel` modes when a custom control server is specified.
- Added tests to ensure correct behavior of the new configuration options and validation rules.
- Updated documentation to reflect changes in Tailscale integration and usage with custom control servers.

Closes #48367

## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: OpenClaw’s Tailscale Gateway flow is currently optimized for the default Tailscale control plane (`serve`/`funnel` workflows).
- Why it matters: Self-hosted users running Headscale must point the Tailscale client to a custom control server (`tailscale up --login-server ...`) and run the Gateway in a compatible mode.
- Fix: add `gateway.tailscale.controlUrl` and `--tailscale-control-url` so custom control servers are explicit and validated.
- Safety: reject unsupported combinations (`controlUrl` + `tailscale.mode=serve|funnel`) with actionable errors.
- Coverage/docs: add focused validation/runtime tests and update Tailscale Gateway docs with Headscale usage and limits.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #48367

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New config key: `gateway.tailscale.controlUrl` (URL).
- New CLI flag: `--tailscale-control-url <url>`.
- Startup/validation now fails fast when `controlUrl` is combined with `tailscale.mode: "serve"` or `"funnel"`.
- URL format is validated for `controlUrl`; invalid values are rejected with clear errors.
- Docs now include Headscale-specific setup and explicit compatibility limits.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes`)  
  - Adds ability to target a custom Tailscale-compatible control server configuration.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)  
  - No new OpenClaw network client path added; this is config/validation/runtime gating.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes`)  
  - New CLI option `--tailscale-control-url`.
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Risk: misconfiguration could lead to unsupported mode selection.
  - Mitigation: hard validation blocks unsupported combinations and returns actionable guidance (`mode=off` + `bind=tailnet`).

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (darwin)
- Runtime/container: Node 22+, pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway + Tailscale (Headscale-compatible control server)
- Relevant config (redacted):
  - `gateway.bind: "tailnet"`
  - `gateway.auth.mode: "token"`
  - `gateway.tailscale.controlUrl: "https://headscale.example.com"`

### Steps

1. Configure `gateway.tailscale.controlUrl` with `tailscale.mode: "off"` (or unset mode) and start gateway.
2. Set `tailscale.mode: "serve"` (then `"funnel"`) with the same `controlUrl` and validate/start again.
3. Pass `--tailscale-control-url not-a-url` and run gateway command.

### Expected

- `controlUrl` + mode `off` (or unset) is accepted.
- `controlUrl` + `serve|funnel` is rejected with clear compatibility error.
- Invalid URL input is rejected immediately.

### Actual

- Matches expected behavior via validation/runtime logic and dedicated tests.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Evidence references:
- `src/config/tailscale-control-url-validation.test.ts`
- `src/gateway/server-runtime-config.test.ts` (`tailscale.controlUrl` cases)
- `src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts` (CLI URL validation and override wiring)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `controlUrl` accepted with `mode=off` and with mode omitted.
  - `controlUrl` rejected with `mode=serve|funnel`.
  - invalid `--tailscale-control-url` rejected by CLI.
- Edge cases checked:
  - `serve`/`funnel` behavior unchanged when `controlUrl` is not set.
  - Config + CLI override paths both enforce URL validity.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Live end-to-end against a real Headscale deployment in this change set.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`Yes`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:
  - Optional adoption only: set `gateway.tailscale.controlUrl` or pass `--tailscale-control-url`.
  - If using custom control server, use `tailscale.mode: "off"` and `gateway.bind: "tailnet"`.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
  - Remove `gateway.tailscale.controlUrl` and run prior/default Tailscale flow.
  - Or keep `controlUrl` and force `tailscale.mode: "off"` with `gateway.bind: "tailnet"`.
- Files/config to restore:
  - Gateway config `gateway.tailscale.*` and CLI invocation flags.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - Startup error mentioning custom control server incompatibility with `serve`/`funnel`.
  - CLI error for invalid `--tailscale-control-url`.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: Users expect `serve`/`funnel` to work with Headscale and view rejection as regression.
  - Mitigation: explicit docs, clear error messages, and recommended supported path (`mode=off` + `bind=tailnet`).
- Risk: Slight DX confusion between Tailscale client login server config and OpenClaw gateway config.
  - Mitigation: docs include both steps and example commands for each layer.

target.title: feat(anthropic): claude-cli-interactive backend — stream reasoning via local TLS proxy (experimental)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** OpenClaw's `claude-cli` backend invokes `claude -p --output-format stream-json` (headless). Starting **June 15, 2026** that path moves onto Anthropic's separate programmatic credit pool ([VentureBeat coverage](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-reinstates-openclaw-and-third-party-agent-usage-on-claude-subscriptions-with-a-catch)) — fixed monthly allowance, billed at API rates, doesn't roll over.
- **Why it matters.** Every OpenClaw Opus turn on a Claude subscription will drain that fixed credit post–June 15 instead of counting against the interactive subscription limits subscribers are paying for. Opus turns exhaust the credit fast.
- **What changed.** New opt-in `claude-cli-interactive` backend. Each turn spawns one fresh `bun wrapper.ts …` that boots a loopback HTTPS MITM on a 127.0.0.1 port, taps every API SSE event, re-emits the events as `claude -p --output-format stream-json` JSONL records, and kills the claude child on `end_turn`. A `tty-spoof.cjs` `NODE_OPTIONS=--require` preload keeps claude in interactive mode under piped stdio.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Existing `claude-cli` backend untouched (shared gate functions — model-capability resolution, OAuth label, credential fingerprint, live-agent probes, model alias detection — extended via a single `isClaudeCliCompatibleBackend(provider)` helper to recognise both backend ids; every gate continues to return identical values for `claude-cli`). `agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli` user overrides inherit into the interactive backend via `inheritUserConfigFrom` with `-p`-mode flags stripped. System CA store is not mutated (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` scoped to the spawned claude child only). Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels pass through unmodified. Tool-use rendering / waiting-on-tool injection lands in #82285, not here.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `claude` running under OpenClaw in interactive (subscription) mode with full `init` / `stream_event*` / `result` JSONL reconstruction on the wrapper's stdout, sourced from the loopback MITM proxy's SSE tap.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, Bun, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142 (npm-installed), Opus 4.7 (`claude-opus-4-7`), live OpenClaw gateway driving the backend per-turn over Telegram.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`; `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/anthropic/tsconfig.json`; set `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` active in user config; restart the OpenClaw gateway; send a Telegram message that triggers an Opus 4.7 turn; observe wrapper boot in stderr, SSE flow through `127.0.0.1:<port>`, JSONL arriving at OpenClaw; send a follow-up message with `--resume <sessionId>` to verify resume.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - vitest: `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 5 passed (5)` — `normalizeConfig` branches (inherited claude-cli match, direct Bun-path override, env passthrough, no-override skip).
  - Gateway log excerpt + cert listing + active-config snapshot on the [`proof-assets/pr-81851`](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/tree/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof) branch. Embedded sample (one of three real turns in the gateway-log excerpt):

    ```
    cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=opus useResume=true session=present
    cli argv: bun <NPM>\…\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose --setting-sources user --resume <SESSION> --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --model opus <prompt:N chars>
    [openclaw-proxy] certs ready: <HOME>\.openclaw\proxy-certs\ca.crt
    [openclaw-proxy] MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081 — Anthropic API traffic intercepted locally to capture thinking_delta events
    [openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: <NPM>\…\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe
    [openclaw-proxy] result flushed, waiting for session write before kill
    [openclaw-proxy] session file updated, killing claude
    ```
- **Observed result after fix:** per-turn lifecycle terminates cleanly — wrapper emits `init`, a continuous stream of `stream_event` records, then a single `result` on `end_turn`, sets `intentionalKill = true`, and `claude.kill()`s the child so the per-turn process tree exits 0.
- **What was not tested:** Linux/macOS (developed on Windows 11 only); bundled-MCP `claude-config-file` mode end-to-end with multiple MCP servers active (only `mcp__openclaw__*` exercised live); forced Anthropic-side `429` / `overloaded_error` mid-stream (the `api_error` synthetic event path is implemented but I did not provoke a real 429).

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Related: #81550 (programmatic-mode reasoning bridge — this PR is the interactive-mode equivalent on a separate backend); #82285 (tool-use injection, builds on the `stream_event` surface this PR establishes).

## Root Cause

N/A — feature PR. The lack of thinking visibility on subscription is a property of `claude -p` (headless) mode, not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** `normalizeConfig` post-merge shape across four branches — inherited claude-cli match (forces `command="bun"`, stashes binary on env), direct Bun-path override (keeps the override as the spawn command, no env stash), env passthrough preservation, no-override case (env passthrough omitted so wrapper falls back to PATH lookup of `"claude"`).
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode is config-shape drift inside `normalizeConfig` — fully exercised synchronously without spawning bun or claude, runs in ms vs the minutes an e2e run would cost.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** newly-installed user with no inherited `claude-cli` override could spawn `bun` as the claude binary if `normalizeConfig` stashed `merged.command` indiscriminately.
  - **Mitigation:** `normalizeConfig` consults `context.config.agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli.command`; only stashes the env passthrough on a match. Regression test asserts the env var is `undefined` for the no-override branch.
- **Risk:** `tty-spoof.cjs` is a runtime support file (not a build output) and could be omitted from packaged installs.
  - **Mitigation:** declared in `extensions/anthropic/package.json#openclaw.build.staticAssets`; postbuild asset copier ships it alongside the wrapper.
- **Risk:** winget-installed `claude.exe` shadows the npm install via PATHEXT on Windows.
  - **Mitigation:** wrapper spawns `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY` directly when the parent backend resolved a binary, never falling back to a bare PATH lookup.
- **Risk:** title-gen / Haiku side-requests inject `{"title":"…"}` artifacts into the reasoning stream.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server classifies each `/v1/messages` request into `auxiliary` (no tools) / `compaction` (compact.ts prompt markers) / `tool_followup` (last msg has tool_result) / `normal`. Wrapper drops auxiliary entirely, streams compaction as `thinking_delta`, emits a result only on `normal` `end_turn`. Response-content fingerprint backup (`<analysis>` + Primary-Request-and-Intent + Pending-Tasks) catches anything that slipped past the request-level classifier.
- **Risk:** spilled-prompt overflow file leaks to concurrent runs via shared tempdir + `--add-dir`.
  - **Mitigation:** per-run subdir under `${tmpdir()}/openclaw-interactive-proxy/run-<32-hex-bytes>/` (0700), file 0600, `--add-dir` scoped to the per-run subdir only.
- **Risk:** local CA + leaf key persisted under user's OpenClaw config dir could be reused if an attacker gets read access.
  - **Mitigation:** trust scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` only (no system trust mutation); CA issues solely the one `api.anthropic.com` leaf the per-turn proxy needs; key files hardened to 0600 with fail-closed regeneration; maintainer security review invited.
- **Risk:** Anthropic-side non-SSE 4xx/5xx on `/v1/messages` (429, billing, overloaded) hangs the wrapper because claude doesn't self-exit on API error.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server emits a synthetic `api_error` event for non-SSE 4xx/5xx; wrapper surfaces a `result` with `is_error: true` and exits nonzero so the CLI runner treats the turn as failed and triggers failover.
- **Risk:** two resumes of the same Claude session race on the same transcript.
  - **Mitigation:** backend config sets `serialize: true` so the framework queues same-session runs; different sessions still run concurrently. mitm-server binds both proxy stages on port 0 so concurrent proxies can't collide on a preferred port either.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** Yes — per-turn local TLS MITM on a loopback port. Scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`; system trust store untouched. Local CA + leaf key cached under user's OpenClaw config dir at 0700/0600.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — wrapper does not read, log, or modify the Anthropic auth token; it is forwarded byte-for-byte to the re-issued upstream request.
- **New/changed network calls:** Yes — claude's outbound HTTPS to `api.anthropic.com:443` is rerouted via `HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>` inside the child. Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) bypass the TLS terminator entirely.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same `claude` binary, same allowed-tools (`mcp__openclaw__*`), same session semantics.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — proxy sees only the SSE bytes claude itself sees on the wire; nothing exfiltrated or aggregated outside the per-turn process.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: existing `claude-cli` backend unchanged. Opt-in via the `claude-cli-interactive` id; no default flips.
- New optional env: `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` enables verbose wrapper stderr.
- Migration needed: no.

## Prior art

[`smithersai/claude-p`](https://github.com/smithersai/claude-p) runs `claude` without `-p` but bypasses the JSONL streaming surface entirely — works for the raw final text but loses the `stream_event` records that reasoning and tool-use rendering depend on. This PR reproduces the `-p --output-format stream-json` shape record-for-record from the SSE tap so existing OpenClaw stream consumers layer on unchanged.

target.title: feat(session): add chat-channel session switching with LRU history retention
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Chat channels did not support listing or switching between prior sessions under the same SessionKey, and follow-up fixes were needed to preserve switchable transcripts until LRU eviction and restore per-session usage/compaction snapshots correctly.
- Why it matters: Without in-chat session switching, users cannot navigate past conversations from Feishu/DingTalk/Telegram/Discord-style chat surfaces; incorrect transcript/archive or snapshot behavior would also make `/session back`, `/sessions`, and `/status`-adjacent state feel unreliable.
- What changed: Added `/sessions`, `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for chat channels; introduced per-SessionKey LRU `sessionHistory`; preserved switchable transcripts until history eviction; restored token/context/compaction snapshots on switch; documented matching/order/retention behavior; cleaned up reset semantics so `session.historyLimit=0` truly disables switchable history.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not change SessionKey routing, does not add cross-SessionKey switching, does not add named sessions/UI picker flows, and does not change channel plugin-specific transport behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #9959
- Related #28168

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

- Added `/sessions` to list switchable sessions for the current chat SessionKey.
- Added `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for in-chat session switching.
- Current session is included in `/sessions`, marked as `[current]`.
- `/sessions` now shows compact single-line preview rows with session ID prefix and truncated preview text.
- Session switching now restores stored token/context/compaction snapshots for the target session.
- `session.historyLimit` now consistently supports `0` as “disable switchable session history”.
- Transcripts for sessions still in history remain switchable; transcripts are archived when evicted from the LRU history queue.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw dev runtime / source tree
- Model/provider: N/A for core session-switch logic
- Integration/channel (if any): chat-channel command flow; manually validated in a real chat session
- Relevant config (redacted):
  - `session.historyLimit: 5` (default path)
  - `session.historyLimit: 0` (disable-history regression path)

### Steps

1. Start a chat conversation and create multiple sessions with `/new`.
2. Run `/sessions`, then switch via `/session <number>` or `/session back`.
3. Continue creating sessions until the history limit is exceeded.

### Expected

- `/sessions` lists current + recent switchable sessions in LRU order.
- Switching restores the target session’s stored metadata/snapshots.
- Sessions still present in history remain switchable.
- The oldest session is archived only after LRU eviction.
- With `session.historyLimit=0`, switchable history is disabled and the previous session is archived immediately on reset.

### Actual

- Behavior matches the expected results above in targeted tests and manual validation.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `/sessions` output in chat
  - `/session back` restores the previous session
  - switching to a still-retained historical session restores expected context/state
  - LRU eviction archives only the oldest evicted session
  - daily reset path preserves switchable history
  - `session.historyLimit=0` disables switchable history and archives immediately on reset
- Edge cases checked:
  - history boundary behavior at the configured limit
  - selecting current session is a no-op
  - unique-prefix SessionId selection behavior
  - stale/daily-reset session rollover
- What you did **not** verify:
  - every individual chat provider/plugin surface
  - cross-SessionKey switching (out of scope)
  - any UI/card-based session picker (out of scope)

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
  - revert this PR, or set `session.historyLimit=0` to disable switchable session history
- Files/config to restore:
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts`
  - `src/config/sessions/types.ts`
  - `src/config/types.base.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - `/sessions` shows stale/incorrect ordering
  - switched sessions lose expected token/context/compaction snapshot state
  - transcripts are archived too early while still present in history
  - `session.historyLimit=0` does not disable switchable history

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: LRU eviction/archive timing could regress and archive a session that should still remain switchable.
  - Mitigation: Added/updated targeted tests covering retained-history vs evicted-history behavior.

- Risk: Snapshot restore could leave token/context/compaction state inconsistent after switching.
  - Mitigation: Added lifecycle tests covering restore and missing-metadata fallback behavior.

- Risk: Docs could drift from implementation again as follow-up fixes land.
  - Mitigation: Updated `docs/design/chat-session-switch.md` to match the current implementation semantics in this PR.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted: Yes

I used an AI assistant for code review, doc cleanup, and targeted test updates. I manually reviewed the final diff, understood the changes, and verified the session-switch flows myself.

target.title: Plugins: consistent conversationId and accountId across hook contexts
target.body:
## Summary

- Pass `conversationId` through directly in `toPluginInboundClaimContext` instead of running it through `deriveConversationId()`, consistent with `toPluginMessageContext` and agent hook contexts
- Add `conversationId` to `PluginHookAgentContext` and wire it through agent runner hook call sites
- Add `accountId` to `PluginHookAgentContext` and `AfterAgentCompleteHookContext`, wire from `params.agentAccountId` and `sessionCtx.AccountId`
- Remove `deriveConversationId` (per-channel normalization should live in channel adapters, not generic hook mappers)

Fixes #49746

## Motivation

Plugin hook contexts produced different `conversationId` values for the same message across hook phases. `inbound_claim` ran the value through `deriveConversationId()` (stripping prefixes, rerouting Discord DMs) while `message_received` and agent hooks passed it through directly. Plugins building channel-scoped state across hook phases (e.g. speedtrap) could never match keys.

Additionally, `accountId` was missing from agent hook contexts entirely, making it impossible for plugins to distinguish per-agent conversations (e.g. DMs to different bot accounts from the same human user).

## Changes

1. **`src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.ts`**: Use `canonical.conversationId` directly in `toPluginInboundClaimContext`. Remove `deriveConversationId`.
2. **`src/plugins/types.ts`**: Add `conversationId` and `accountId` to `PluginHookAgentContext`.
3. **`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts`**: Wire `conversationId` and `accountId` into hookCtx.
4. **`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts`**: Wire `conversationId` and `accountId` into hookCtx.
5. **`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-hooks.ts`**: Add `accountId` to `AfterAgentCompleteHookContext`, pass through to hook context.
6. **`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts`**: Supply `accountId` from `sessionCtx.AccountId`.

## Test plan

- [x] `src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.test.ts` updated and passing (7/7)
- [ ] Verify Discord DM routing is not affected by `deriveConversationId` removal (the old derivation converted raw Discord targets to `user:` prefixed form for DMs)
- [ ] Manual testing with multi-agent Slack channels confirms consistent keys across all hook phases

## Note on Discord

The removed `deriveConversationId` did per-channel transformations including Discord DM target rerouting. The test was updated to expect the raw `conversationId` passthrough. Discord DM routing should be verified to ensure no regression.

target.title: fix(nextcloud-talk): parse rich object file shares from webhook payload
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #29152

When a user shares a file or image in Nextcloud Talk, the bot webhook receives the message but the NC Talk plugin was not parsing the rich object parameters — so the agent never saw the attachment.

NC Talk encodes file shares as rich object messages where `object.content` is a JSON string:
```json
{"message":"{file}","parameters":{"file":{"type":"file","id":"12345","name":"IMG_123.jpg","path":"Talk/IMG_123.jpg","link":"https://cloud.example.com/f/12345","mimetype":"image/jpeg","preview-available":"yes"}}}
```

Previously `payloadToInboundMessage()` passed this raw JSON string as `text`, leaving `mediaUrls` unpopulated.

## Changes

### `monitor.ts`
- Adds `parseRichObjectContent()` helper that detects JSON-encoded rich object payloads
- Extracts file links into `mediaUrls` (https/http only — rejects `file://`, `javascript:`, etc.)
- Resolves `{placeholder}` names for human-readable `displayText` (covers file shares, mentions, deck cards, polls, etc.)
- Uses `replaceAll` for placeholder substitution to handle duplicate occurrences
- Falls back to raw `content` for non-rich-object text — no behaviour change for regular messages

### `types.ts`
- Adds `mediaUrls?: string[]` to `NextcloudTalkInboundMessage`

### `inbound.ts`
- Passes `MediaUrls` into `finalizeInboundContext` (verified: already consumed natively as `string[]`)
- Relaxes the empty-body guard to allow media-only messages through (previously silently dropped)

## Tests

- `monitor.attachment.test.ts` — 7 unit tests: file share, text passthrough, malformed JSON fallback, name fallback, mention resolution, `file://` URL rejection, multi-file
- `monitor.live-integration.test.ts` — 5 integration tests using real HMAC-signed payloads against the webhook server

All 12 tests pass. TypeScript compiles clean (`tsc --noEmit`).

## Before / After

**Before:** Sharing a file in NC Talk → agent receives raw JSON string, never sees the attachment.

**After:** Sharing a file → agent receives `text = "filename.jpg"` and `mediaUrls = ["https://..."]` — can immediately process the image via vision without any manual workaround.

target.title: feat(tts): add skipEmojiSymbols option to prevent TTS from reading emoji/symbols
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the opt-in `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` TTS preprocessing setting.
- Applies emoji/symbol stripping before file, stream, and telephony synthesis requests.
- Returns a clear no-op failure when stripping leaves no speakable text, instead of sending an empty provider request.
- Keeps default behavior unchanged when the option is disabled or unset.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Refs #77969
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

TTS can turn emoji and decorative symbols into noisy spoken names. Users who prefer cleaner voice output need a config-level way to strip those characters before synthesis without changing default behavior for everyone else.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: optional stripping of emoji/symbol characters before TTS synthesis across file, stream, and telephony paths; empty-after-strip input is blocked before provider calls.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, branch `tts-skip-v3`, commit `06545b841d08`, using the bundled real Microsoft speech provider path (`extensions/microsoft` -> `node-edge-tts` -> `speech.platform.bing.com`). No API keys or private account secrets were used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... register microsoft plugin, call textToSpeech twice with skipEmojiSymbols=false/true, then call an emoji-only enabled case ... TS`
  - `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
  - `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- After-fix evidence:
  ```text
  $ node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # Registers the bundled Microsoft Speech plugin and performs real synthesis
  # through node-edge-tts / speech.platform.bing.com after this patch.
  TS
  {
    "branch": "tts-skip-v3",
    "commit": "06545b841d08",
    "provider": "microsoft",
    "realProviderPath": "extensions/microsoft -> node-edge-tts -> speech.platform.bing.com",
    "inputText": "Proof phrase: package ready. Check mark ✓ copyright © melting face 🫠 done.",
    "observed": [
      {
        "name": "disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": false,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
        "target": "audio-file",
        "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false.mp3",
        "bytes": 39456,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      },
      {
        "name": "enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": true,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
        "target": "audio-file",
        "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true.mp3",
        "bytes": 36288,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      }
    ],
    "emptyAfterStrip": {
      "success": false,
      "error": "TTS text is empty after removing emoji and symbol characters",
      "provider": null
    },
    "artifactsDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof"
  }

  $ ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration,size ...
  ffprobe was not installed in this container, so artifact validation used byte size plus MP3 frame header checks.

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       32 passed (32)

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       14 passed (14)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: disabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio from the original text containing `✓`, `©`, and `🫠`; enabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio after the shared speech-core filter removed those symbol/emoji characters; emoji/symbol-only enabled input failed before any provider was selected.
- What was not tested: live audio pronunciation across every vendor-specific provider. The real-provider proof uses the bundled Microsoft speech provider because it is credential-free and exercises the same speech-core preprocessing path that all providers share.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

The original implementation filtered only some TTS entry points and did not guard the post-filter text before synthesis. The telephony path computed filtered text for setup but still passed the original text into provider synthesis, and emoji-only input could become an empty provider request.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`

text -> effective TTS config -> optional emoji/symbol strip -> empty guard -> provider synthesis

target.title: feat(heartbeat): add maxCostPerRun config to cap embedded run cost
target.body:
## Problem

A single embedded run on an expensive model with a large context has no cost bound. Existing fixes (trigger dedup, retry guards) reduce how often runs fire but do not cap individual run cost. See #49823, #3181.

## Solution

New optional config option `maxCostPerRun` (USD float, default: none). Before calling `getReplyFromConfig()` in `runHeartbeatOnce()`, estimated cost is computed from context size and model pricing. If the estimate exceeds the cap, the run returns `{ status: "skipped", reason: "cost-cap-exceeded" }` without invoking the model.

Implemented at the dispatch level so it covers heartbeat, cron, and future trigger types.

## How it works

- Token count estimated from `ctx.Body` character length (chars / 4, conservative)
- Model pricing resolved from the config model catalog (`resolveModelCostConfig`) first, then a hardcoded fallback table, then a conservative $15/M default for unknown models
- Provider-prefixed refs (`openai/gpt-4o`, `openrouter/anthropic/claude-opus-4`) handled via `lastIndexOf("/")` stripping
- Free/local models with `cost.input = 0` in the catalog estimate correctly at $0
- Check inserted between `replyOpts` construction and `getReplyFromConfig` call
- Skipped runs emit a warning log with estimated cost and cap value

## Config example

```yaml
heartbeat:
  every: "120m"
  maxCostPerRun: 0.50
```

## Testing

29 tests (15 unit + 7 integration + 7 edge case):

- Pricing: known models, cheap models, unknown model fallback, empty/single-char prompt, 128K context
- Prefix ordering: gpt-4o vs gpt-4, gpt-4-turbo vs gpt-4, o1-mini vs o1, o3-mini vs o3
- Catalog: catalog hit, catalog miss, zero cost (free model), negative cost, bare model name, provider-prefixed, nested provider prefix
- Case insensitivity
- Integration: cap exceeded (getReplyFromConfig not called), cap within (called), cap unset (backward compat), cap=0 (deny all), negative/NaN/Infinity (ignored), free model + cap=0

## Non-goals

Not a global budget system. Caps individual runs only. Step limits and transcript-based estimation are out of scope.

Fixes #49823
Ref #3181

target.title: fix: retry same auth profile on transient overloaded errors before failover
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** When the LLM API returns a transient `overloaded` error (HTTP 529 / `overloaded_error`), OpenClaw tries to rotate to the next auth profile. If only one profile is configured for the provider, the error is surfaced immediately with "The AI service is temporarily overloaded. Please try again in a moment." — no retry.
- **Why it matters:** During Anthropic capacity spikes (frequent in March 2026), agents with a single API key become unresponsive. Users must manually resend messages. The cron subsystem already has configurable retry (`cron.retry`), but the main LLM request path does not.
- **What changed:** Added a same-profile retry loop (up to 3 attempts, exponential backoff 2s→4s→8s, jitter 25%, capped at 30s) for `overloaded` errors when no other profile is available. Applies to both prompt-side and assistant-side error paths.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Existing profile rotation, fallback model logic, and backoff-before-failover behavior are untouched. Rate-limit, auth, billing, and format errors are NOT retried. The retry counter resets on success. No new config surface (hardcoded constants — a follow-up could expose these via `agents.defaults.llmRetry`).

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #49376
- Closes #48913
- Related #49696
- Related #24321

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- When a single-profile provider returns `overloaded` (529), the agent now silently retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff (2s, 4s, 8s) before surfacing the error.
- Log messages at `warn` level: `overloaded — retrying same profile for <provider>/<model>: attempt=N/3 delayMs=X`.
- Failover observation logs a new decision value: `retry_same_profile`.
- If all 3 retries are exhausted, behavior is identical to before (fallback model or surface error).

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No` (same LLM API call, just retried)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (Darwin arm64)
- Runtime: Node 24
- Model/provider: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.6 (single API key profile)
- Channel: Telegram

### Steps

1. Configure an agent with a single Anthropic auth profile (no fallbacks).
2. Send a message when Anthropic API is overloaded (returns 529).
3. **Before fix:** Error surfaced immediately.
4. **After fix:** Agent retries up to 3 times with backoff, then surfaces error only if still overloaded.

### Expected

Agent retries transparently and recovers when the API comes back within ~15s.

### Actual

Agent retries (visible in warn logs) and succeeds on retry, or surfaces error after 3 failed attempts.

## Evidence

- `tsgo` type check: 0 errors ✅
- `oxlint` on changed files: 0 warnings, 0 errors ✅
- `vitest run` on `failover-observation.test.ts`: 2/2 passed ✅
- `vitest run` on `runs.test.ts`: 6/6 passed ✅

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified: TypeScript compiles, lint passes, existing tests pass.
- Verified: Code review of both prompt-side and assistant-side paths — retry only triggers for `overloaded` reason, counter resets on success and on profile rotation.
- Edge cases checked: abort signal interrupts retry sleep; counter does not interfere with existing `overloadFailoverAttempts`; `markAuthProfileGood` is called before retry to clear cooldown state.
- What I did **not** verify: Live 529 error from Anthropic (cannot reproduce on demand).

## Review Conversations

- [x] N/A (new PR)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- Revert this single commit to restore previous behavior (immediate surface on overloaded).
- The retry is bounded (3 attempts, ~15s max wall-clock) and respects abort signals, so it cannot cause infinite loops.
- Watch for: warn-level log spam if overload is persistent (3 log lines per failed request vs 0 before).

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Retry adds up to ~15s latency before surfacing a persistent overload error.
  - Mitigation: 3 retries with 2s/4s/8s backoff is a reasonable tradeoff. Users previously had to manually retry anyway. A future config option could let users tune this.
- Risk: `markAuthProfileGood` call before retry could mask a genuinely bad profile.
  - Mitigation: Only called for `overloaded` reason (transient), not for auth/billing/format errors. The profile was working before the overload.

target.title: docs/types: clarify that omitting accountId in bindings matches default account only, not all accounts
target.body:
## Summary

- **Fix**: Auto-set `accountId: "*"` when a channel has multiple accounts configured and no explicit accountId is provided during `openclaw agents add` / `openclaw agents bind`
- **Docs**: Add JSDoc to `AgentBindingMatch.accountId` documenting the three behaviors (omitted → default only, `"*"` → all, string → specific)
- **Docs**: Replace easy-to-miss plain text note in `multi-agent.md` with a prominent `[!WARNING]` callout
- **Docs**: Add `--bind` format reference table and `agents add --bind` examples to CLI docs
- **Docs**: Update binding examples to include `accountId: "*"` as the safe default

## Problem

When configuring multi-agent routing with `bindings` in `openclaw.json`, omitting the `accountId` field silently matches **only the default account** rather than all accounts. This is counter-intuitive and causes hard-to-debug routing failures when multiple channel accounts are configured (e.g. WhatsApp `personal` + `biz`, Telegram `default` + `alerts`).

### Scenario

A user configures two accounts on a channel and sets up bindings to route specific groups to specific agents:

```json
{
  "channels": {
    "telegram": {
      "accounts": {
        "default": { "botToken": "123456:ABC..." },
        "alerts": { "botToken": "987654:XYZ..." }
      }
    }
  },
  "bindings": [
    {
      "match": {
        "channel": "telegram",
        "peer": { "kind": "group", "id": "-100123456789" }
      },
      "agentId": "work"
    }
  ]
}
```

**Expected**: The binding routes messages from the group to the `work` agent regardless of which account receives the message.

**Actual**:
- Messages via `telegram[default]` → ✅ correctly dispatched to `work` agent
- Messages via `telegram[alerts]` → ❌ silently falls back to `main` agent

### Root cause (in `src/routing/resolve-route.ts`)

`resolveAccountPatternKey()` converts an empty `accountPattern` (from an omitted `accountId`) to the literal string `"default"`:

```typescript
function resolveAccountPatternKey(accountPattern: string): string {
  if (!accountPattern.trim()) {
    return DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID;  // → "default"
  }
  return normalizeAccountId(accountPattern);
}
```

During `buildEvaluatedBindingsByChannel()`, bindings without `accountId` are indexed into the `byAccount.get("default")` bucket. Only bindings with `accountId: "*"` enter `byAnyAccount`.

At lookup time in `getEvaluatedBindingsForChannelAccount()`:

```typescript
const accountScoped = channelBindings?.byAccount.get(accountId) ?? [];
const anyAccount = channelBindings?.byAnyAccount ?? [];
```

When a message arrives from a non-default account, the system queries `byAccount.get("alerts")` — which is empty. `byAnyAccount` is also empty. No binding matches → fallback to default agent.

### Why this is confusing

1. Most users expect "field omitted" = "match any / don't care", not "match only default"
2. The `openclaw agents add` wizard does **not** prompt for `accountId` when creating bindings, even when multiple accounts are configured
3. Many doc examples showed bindings without `accountId`, implying this is the normal pattern
4. The TypeScript type `AgentBindingMatch.accountId` had no JSDoc explaining the default behavior
5. The CLI docs had no `--bind` format reference or examples showing the `:*` wildcard syntax

### Related issues

- #26066 — Feishu accountId binding not working
- #17817 — Telegram multi-account: accountId in bindings ignored
- #26421 — Feishu Channel Binding Routing Not Working
- #26199 — Channel agentId 配置未生效

## Changes

### 1. `src/commands/agents.bindings.ts` — CLI behavior fix (core fix)

In `resolveBindingAccountId()`, when a channel has **multiple accounts** configured and no explicit `accountId` is provided (by the user or plugin), the function now returns `"*"` instead of `undefined`. This ensures that `openclaw agents add` and `openclaw agents bind` automatically create bindings that match all accounts on multi-account channels.

The fix is safe and backward-compatible:
- If user explicitly specifies accountId (e.g. `--bind telegram:alerts`), that is used (no change)
- If plugin provides its own `resolveBindingAccountId` hook, that is used (no change)
- If plugin has `forceAccountBinding`, the default account is used (no change)
- **NEW**: If channel has >1 accounts, `"*"` is returned (fixes the issue)
- If channel has only 1 account, `undefined` is returned (no change)

### 2. `src/config/types.agents.ts` — Type annotation

Added JSDoc to `AgentBindingMatch.accountId` explicitly documenting all three behaviors (omitted → default only, `"*"` → wildcard, string → specific account).

### 3. `docs/concepts/multi-agent.md` — Core routing docs

- Replaced the easy-to-miss plain text "Important account-scope detail" with a prominent `> [!WARNING]` callout block
- Updated "WhatsApp daily chat + Telegram deep work" example bindings to include `accountId: "*"`
- Updated "same channel, one peer to Opus" example bindings to include `accountId: "*"`
- Added `> [!TIP]` explaining that `"*"` is always safe even for single-account setups

### 4. `docs/cli/agents.md` — CLI reference

- Added `> [!WARNING]` callout to the "Binding scope behavior" section
- Added `--bind` format reference table (`:*`, `:<account>`, bare channel)
- Added example showing `agents add --bind` at creation time
- Added wildcard and specific-account examples to the bind examples section

## Test plan

- [ ] Run `openclaw agents add` with a channel that has multiple accounts — verify the created binding includes `accountId: "*"`
- [ ] Run `openclaw agents bind --bind telegram` with multiple telegram accounts — verify `accountId: "*"` is set
- [ ] Run `openclaw agents bind --bind telegram:alerts` — verify explicit accountId is preserved (not overridden to `"*"`)
- [ ] Run `openclaw agents add` with a single-account channel — verify no `accountId` is added (same behavior as before)
- [ ] Verify JSDoc renders correctly in IDE hover tooltips
- [ ] Verify `[!WARNING]` / `[!TIP]` callouts render properly on docs.openclaw.ai

## Summary

Add ⏱ response duration display to the per-reply usage footer so users can see how long each AI response took to generate.

## Changes

- **`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.ts`**: Add optional `durationMs` parameter to `formatResponseUsageLine()`, format as `⏱ X.Xs` and insert between tokens and cost
- **`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts`**: Compute `durationMs = Date.now() - runStartedAt` once, reuse for both diagnostic events and usage footer

## Before / After

```
Before:  Usage: 8.1k in / 2.4k out · est $0.001
After:   Usage: 8.1k in / 2.4k out / ⏱ 3.2s / est $0.001
```

## Design Decisions

- Duration covers the full agent turn (including tool calls)
- For pure model-only responses it reflects actual generation time
- Reuses the same `Date.now() - runStartedAt` computation already used by diagnostic events
- Fully backward-compatible: `durationMs` is optional, omitting it preserves existing behavior exactly

target.title: fix(sessions): preserve slack thread routing for A2A sessions
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes a related A2A routing regression for Slack thread sessions.

Two issues were contributing to the failure mode:

1. `resolveGroupSessionKey()` could normalize agent-prefixed Slack thread keys such as `agent:builder:slack:channel:C123:thread:...` as `webchat` because it only looked at the first segment of `ctx.From`.
2. Restart/announce fallback could still pull delivery routing back toward the base session when the current session route was only partially populated.

## Changes

- Teach `resolveGroupSessionKey()` to extract the real provider from `agent:<id>:<provider>:...` keys.
- Only trust restart sentinel `deliveryContext` when it is complete (`channel` + `to`).
- Use the base session only as a field-level fallback when the current session delivery context is incomplete.
- Add regression coverage for:
  - agent-prefixed Slack thread session keys
  - incomplete current delivery context + base-session fallback

## Why this matters

Without this fix, A2A Slack thread sessions can be persisted as `webchat`, and subsequent announce/restart delivery can drift back to the wrong thread/session.

## Related upstream context

- #44153
- #45929
- #44548

This PR addresses a related but distinct root cause that showed up in Slack-thread A2A routing.

## Tests

- `pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.unit.config.ts src/config/sessions/group.test.ts`

target.title: feat(device-pairing): add structured error objects instead of null for scope escalation
target.body:
## Summary

This PR modifies the device pairing token system to return structured error objects instead of `null` or legacy error formats when scope escalation is detected or other error conditions occur. This provides better error information and type safety throughout the codebase.

## Changes Made

### Core Infrastructure (`src/infra/device-pairing.ts`)

**Type System Updates:**
- Extended `RotateDeviceTokenResult` type to include error object variant:
  ```typescript
  | {
      error: "SCOPE_ESCALATION_DETECTED";
      message: string;
      details: { role: string; requestedScopes: string[]; approvedScopes: string[] | null };
    }
  ```

**Function Changes:**
- **`ensureDeviceToken()`**: Changed return type from `DeviceAuthToken | null` to `DeviceAuthToken | { error: string; message: string; details: Record<string, unknown> }`
  - Returns `DEVICE_OR_ROLE_NOT_FOUND` error when device or role is missing
  - Returns `SCOPE_ESCALATION_DETECTED` error when requested scopes exceed approved baseline
  - Returns `DeviceAuthToken` on success

- **`rotateDeviceToken()`**: Now returns structured error object for scope escalation:
  - Previously: `{ ok: false, reason: "scope-outside-approved-baseline" }`
  - Now: `{ error: "SCOPE_ESCALATION_DETECTED", message: "...", details: {...} }`

- **`revokeDeviceToken()`**: Changed return type to return error objects instead of `null`:
  - Returns `DEVICE_NOT_FOUND` when device is missing
  - Returns `ROLE_NOT_FOUND` when role is invalid
  - Returns `TOKEN_NOT_FOUND` when token is missing
  - Returns `DeviceAuthToken` on success

### Gateway Server Methods (`src/gateway/server-methods/devices.ts`)

- Updated `deviceHandlers.rotateToken()` to handle error objects from `rotateDeviceToken()`
- Updated `deviceHandlers.revokeToken()` to handle error objects from `revokeDeviceToken()`
- Enhanced `logDeviceTokenRotationDenied()` to accept broader error types including detailed error objects
- Added error object handling with proper logging of scope escalation details

### WebSocket Message Handler (`src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts`)

- Updated device token retrieval to handle error objects from `ensureDeviceToken()`
- Added detailed logging for scope escalation detection with context (connId, deviceId, role, scopes, error details)
- Properly rejects handshake when scope escalation is detected

### Test Updates

All test files updated to handle the new error structure:

- `src/infra/device-pairing.test.ts`: Updated `requireRotatedEntry()` helper, rotation tests, and scope escalation tests
- `src/gateway/server.auth.compat-baseline.test.ts`: Added error handling for rotation results
- `src/gateway/server.device-pair-approve-authz.test.ts`: Added error handling for rotation results
- `src/gateway/server.device-token-rotate-authz.test.ts`: Added error handling for rotation results

## Error Object Structure

When scope escalation is detected, the following error object is returned:

```typescript
{
  error: "SCOPE_ESCALATION_DETECTED",
  message: "Requested scopes exceed approved baseline",
  details: {
    role: string,
    requestedScopes: string[],
    approvedScopes: string[] | null
  }
}
```

## Benefits

1. **Better Error Information**: Clients now receive detailed error information including the specific scope escalation reason and context
2. **Type Safety**: TypeScript now properly enforces error handling for all possible return types
3. **Consistent Error Pattern**: Follows existing error patterns in the codebase
4. **Improved Debugging**: Error details provide context for troubleshooting scope-related issues

## Debugging Improvements

### Before (returned `null`)
```
[DEBUG] Device token rotation denied
  Device ID: device-12345
  Role: operator
  Reason: scope-outside-approved-baseline
```

### After (returns detailed error object)
```
[DEBUG] Device token rotation denied
  Device ID: device-12345
  Role: operator
  Requested Scopes: ["operator.admin", "operator.write"]
  Approved Scopes: ["operator.read"]
  Error: SCOPE_ESCALATION_DETECTED
  Message: Requested scopes exceed approved baseline
  Details: {
    "role": "operator",
    "requestedScopes": ["operator.admin", "operator.write"],
    "approvedScopes": ["operator.read"]
  }
```

## Testing

- ✅ TypeScript type checking passes
- ✅ Project builds successfully
- ✅ All existing tests updated to handle new error structure
- ✅ Error handling verified in gateway message handler

## Migration Guide

This is a **breaking change** for any code that directly calls `ensureDeviceToken()` and expects a `null` return value. Callers should be updated to check for error objects:

```typescript
// Before
const token = await ensureDeviceToken(params);
if (token === null) {
  // Handle error case
  return;
}

// After
const result = await ensureDeviceToken(params);
if ("error" in result) {
  // Handle error case
  console.error(result.message);
  return;
}
// Continue with successful DeviceAuthToken result
```

## Related Issues

- Fixes scope escalation detection and reporting
- Improves error handling consistency across the device pairing system

## Additional Notes

- All changes maintain backward compatibility for successful token operations
- Error responses in the gateway are properly formatted using existing `errorShape()` utility

target.title: fix: add circuit breaker to proxy fetch to prevent CPU exhaustion
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds a circuit breaker (`proxy-probe.ts`) that opens on proxy connection errors (ECONNREFUSED, ETIMEDOUT, etc.) and falls back to direct fetch
- Exponential backoff cooldown (10s → 5min) before re-probing; auto-resumes when proxy recovers
- Wraps `makeProxyFetch`, `resolveProxyFetchFromEnv`, Discord REST fetch, and Discord gateway (WS + fetch) with the circuit breaker
- Reduces Discord gateway `maxAttempts` from 50 to 10

## Problem
When `HTTP_PROXY` points to an unavailable proxy, every channel (Discord, Telegram, etc.) independently retries connections through the dead proxy. The cumulative effect of rapid connection failures across multiple channels causes excessive CPU usage.

## Test plan
- [x] 17 unit tests for circuit breaker state machine + `isProxyConnectError`
- [x] 4 integration tests for fetch circuit breaker (fallback, rethrow, skip-after-open, resume-after-cooldown)
- [x] All 84 affected tests pass (net infra + Discord monitor)

target.title: Audit config backups for plaintext secrets
target.body:
## Summary

/claim #11829

This closes a concrete remaining gap from the API-key protection roadmap: stale adjacent `openclaw.json` backups and managed snapshots can still contain plaintext credentials after the active config has moved to SecretRefs. `openclaw secrets audit` now scans those adjacent config artifacts and reports plaintext credential targets found there.

## Changes

- Add `listConfigBackupPaths()` coverage for rotation backups plus managed adjacent snapshots: `openclaw.json.bak`, `openclaw.json.bak.*`, `openclaw.json.*.bak`, `openclaw.json.pre-update`, `openclaw.json.rejected.*`, and `openclaw.json.clobbered.*`.
- Parse backup/snapshot files with config-compatible JSON5 semantics instead of strict `JSON.parse`.
- Report unreadable, oversized, or unparsable backup snapshots as audit findings instead of silently treating them as clean.
- Report plaintext findings with the backup/snapshot file path and JSON path so users can clean stale copies.
- Add regression coverage for `.bak`, managed adjacent snapshots, and JSON5-authored backups.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw secrets audit --json` reports plaintext provider secrets from adjacent config backup/snapshot files while the active config uses an env SecretRef.

Real environment tested: local source checkout on Node.js 22.22.0 with a temporary OpenClaw state directory at `$PWD/tmp/cli-proof/.openclaw`. The active `openclaw.json` used `apiKey: { source: "env", provider: "default", id: "OPENAI_API_KEY" }`. Adjacent stale snapshots contained redacted plaintext keys in `models.providers.openai.apiKey`: `openclaw.json.bak` with JSON5 comments/trailing commas, `openclaw.json.pre-update`, `openclaw.json.rejected.20260228T010203Z`, and `openclaw.json.clobbered.20260228T010203Z`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
rm -rf tmp/cli-proof
mkdir -p tmp/cli-proof/.openclaw
node -e 'write active SecretRef config plus adjacent stale snapshots'
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$PWD/tmp/cli-proof/.openclaw" \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$PWD/tmp/cli-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json" \
OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-redacted-live-env-key" \
pnpm exec tsx src/entry.ts secrets audit --json
```

Evidence after fix: redacted terminal output from the real CLI command above:

```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "status": "findings",
  "resolution": {
    "refsChecked": 1,
    "skippedExecRefs": 0,
    "resolvabilityComplete": true
  },
  "filesScanned": [
    "$PWD/tmp/cli-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json",
    "$PWD/tmp/cli-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak",
    "$PWD/tmp/cli-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json.clobbered.20260228T010203Z",
    "$PWD/tmp/cli-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json.pre-update",
    "$PWD/tmp/cli-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json.rejected.20260228T010203Z"
  ],
  "summary": {
    "plaintextCount": 4,
    "unresolvedRefCount": 0,
    "shadowedRefCount": 0,
    "legacyResidueCount": 0
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "code": "PLAINTEXT_FOUND",
      "severity": "warn",
      "file": "$PWD/tmp/cli-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak",
      "jsonPath": "models.providers.openai.apiKey",
      "message": "models.providers.openai.apiKey is stored as plaintext in a config backup.",
      "provider": "openai"
    },
    {
      "code": "PLAINTEXT_FOUND",
      "severity": "warn",
      "file": "$PWD/tmp/cli-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json.clobbered.20260228T010203Z",
      "jsonPath": "models.providers.openai.apiKey",
      "message": "models.providers.openai.apiKey is stored as plaintext in a config backup.",
      "provider": "openai"
    },
    {
      "code": "PLAINTEXT_FOUND",
      "severity": "warn",
      "file": "$PWD/tmp/cli-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json.pre-update",
      "jsonPath": "models.providers.openai.apiKey",
      "message": "models.providers.openai.apiKey is stored as plaintext in a config backup.",
      "provider": "openai"
    },
    {
      "code": "PLAINTEXT_FOUND",
      "severity": "warn",
      "file": "$PWD/tmp/cli-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json.rejected.20260228T010203Z",
      "jsonPath": "models.providers.openai.apiKey",
      "message": "models.providers.openai.apiKey is stored as plaintext in a config backup.",
      "provider": "openai"
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: The real CLI output shows `status: findings`, scans the active config plus all four adjacent backup/snapshot files, reports `plaintextCount: 4`, and emits one `PLAINTEXT_FOUND` finding for each stale adjacent config artifact. The active config SecretRef is resolvable (`refsChecked: 1`, `unresolvedRefCount: 0`).

What was not tested: I did not run a packaged npm install smoke for `openclaw secrets audit`; this proof uses the source-checkout CLI path via `pnpm exec tsx src/entry.ts secrets audit --json`. I also did not test Windows filesystem snapshot names manually beyond the repository test suite.

## Validation

```text
pnpm exec vitest run src/secrets/audit.test.ts -t "adjacent config"

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       3 passed | 23 skipped (26)
```

```text
pnpm exec vitest run src/secrets/audit.test.ts -t "JSON5 semantics"

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       1 passed | 25 skipped (26)
```

```text
pnpm exec vitest run src/secrets/audit.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       26 passed (26)
```

`git diff --check` passes.

target.title: fix(tui): preserve spaces between thinking fragments
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: streamed thinking fragments were joined with single newlines, and the TUI markdown renderer collapsed those boundaries so adjacent sentences appeared smushed together
- Why it matters: reasoning output in the terminal became hard to read even though the underlying content arrived correctly
- What changed: normalize single-line thinking boundaries into spaces while preserving paragraph breaks, and add formatter/stream-assembler regressions
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): assistant answer text rendering and non-thinking text block joining remain unchanged

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #66008
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the TUI formatter preserved single newlines between thinking fragments, but the markdown renderer did not render those boundaries as readable spaces
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no regression test covering multiple thinking blocks rendered in a single streamed assistant update
- Contributing context (if known): reasoning/thinking often arrives in sentence-sized chunks instead of one complete paragraph

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: src/tui/tui-formatters.test.ts and src/tui/tui-stream-assembler.test.ts
- Scenario the test should lock in: consecutive thinking fragments stay readable with spaces while explicit paragraph breaks survive
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the exact formatter and streamed-assembly surfaces that build terminal assistant output
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): prior tests covered thinking visibility but not fragment boundary spacing
- If no new test is added, why not:

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Streamed thinking text in the TUI now keeps sentence spacing instead of smushing adjacent fragments together.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[thinking chunk A]\n[thinking chunk B] -> markdown render -> "chunk Achunk B"

After:
[thinking chunk A]\n[thinking chunk B] -> whitespace normalization -> "chunk A chunk B"
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (No)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No)
- New/changed network calls? (No)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
- Data access scope changed? (No)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local pnpm/vitest
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): TUI
- Relevant config (redacted): showThinking=true

### Steps

1. Render an assistant message containing multiple thinking fragments.
2. Show thinking in the TUI.
3. Inspect the rendered output.

### Expected

- Adjacent thinking fragments remain readable with spaces between sentences.

### Actual

- Before this fix, sentence boundaries could collapse and appear as one long smushed block.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: targeted vitest run for src/tui/tui-formatters.test.ts and src/tui/tui-stream-assembler.test.ts
- Edge cases checked: consecutive thinking fragments and explicit paragraph breaks
- What you did **not** verify: full interactive terminal reproduction against a live model stream

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (Yes)
- Config/env changes? (No)
- Migration needed? (No)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: some intentionally line-broken thinking text could become single-paragraph prose
  - Mitigation: explicit blank-line paragraph breaks are preserved, and the fix only touches thinking text in the TUI path
